name: cforces
documentation:
[![Built With][BuiltWithPy-Badge]][BuiltWithPy-Link] [![Stargazers][Stars-Badge]][Stars-Link] [![License][License-Badge]][License-Link] [![Telegram Channel][Telegram-Badge]][Telegram-Link]

Python Codeforces API

A Codeforces API wrapper for Python.
Report Bug · Request Feature

## About The Project `cfapi` is a complete implementation of the [Codeforces-API][CodeforcesAPI-Link], featuring classes for every type of the API, each with detailed documentation for a better understanding. It's heavily inspired on [Pyrogram][Pyrogram-Link] structure and documentation.

(back to top)

## Installation ### From PyPi (stable) ```sh pip install cforces ``` ### From Github (latest) ``` pip install git+https://github.com/x93bd0/cfapi/ ```

(back to top)

## Basic Usage As an example, in the following code snippet we are obtaining the handles of the friends of the authenticated user and printing their user objects: ```python from cforces import Client, types from typing import List import asyncio async def main() -> None: client: Client = Client() # Instantiate a Client object. client.auth( YOUR_API_KEY, YOUR_API_SECRET ) # Authenticate yourself with your api_key + api_secret. async with client as api: friends_handles: List[str] = ( await api.user_friends() ) # Fetch authenticated user's friends. friends_users: List[User] = await api.user_info( friends_handles ) # Fetch a user object for each of those. for user in friends_users: print(user) # Print it. if __name__ == "__main__": asyncio.run(main()) ```

(back to top)

## TODO List - [ ] Finish the documentation. - [ ] Implement helper methods for making the interaction easier. - [ ] Find every possible error and implement it. - [ ] Add tests. ## License Distributed under the MIT License. See `LICENSE` for more information.

(back to top)

methods.name: cforces.methods.blog_entry.BlogEntry.blog_entry_comments
documentation:
Retrieves every comment associated to a specific blog entry.

```
param blog_entry_id

ID of the target blog entry.
It can be seen in the blog entry URL. For example:
/blog/entry/79,
where the 79 represents the ID of the blog entry.

return

The comments associated with the blog entry.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.blog_entry.BlogEntry.blog_entry_view
documentation:
Retrieves a blog entry object in its full version.

```
param blog_entry_id

ID of the target blog entry.
It can be seen in the blog entry URL. For example:
/blog/entry/79,
where the 79 represents the ID of the blog entry.

return

A blog entry object in its full version.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.problemset.ProblemSet.problemset_problems
documentation:
Retrieves problems (and their statistics) from a problemset.

```
param tags

Problem tags.

param problemset_name

Short name of the problemset the problem belongs to.

return

A tuple of Problem's and ProblemStatistics's
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.problemset.ProblemSet.problemset_recent_status
documentation:
Retrieves recent submissions from a problemset.

```
param count

Number of submissions to return.

param problemset_name

Short name of the problemset the problem belongs to.

return

A list of submissions belonging to the specified problemset.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.recent_actions.RecentActions.recent_actions
documentation:
Retrieves public recent actions in the platform.

```
param max_count

Maximum count of recent actions to be retrieved.
(Can be up to 100)

return

A list of recent actions.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.user.User.user_blog_entries
documentation:
Retrieves blog entries of a specific user.

```
param handle

User's codeforces handle.

return

A list of blog entries.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.user.User.user_friends
documentation:
Retrieves friends the friends of an authenticated user.

```
param only_online

If this parameter is true, then the API will only
return the friends of the authenticated user who are online.

raises errors.Unauthorized

Raised if the user is not authenticated.

return

A list of handles.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.user.User.user_info
documentation:
Retrieves the user information bounded to a list of handles.

```
param handles

List of handles. The API will not return information
for more than 10000 handles.

param check_historic_handles

If this parameter is true, then the API
will also use the information of any historic handle changes to
search for users.

return

A list of users.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.user.User.user_rated_list
documentation:
Retrieves users that have been in, at least, one rated contest.

```
param active_only

Include only users that have participated in a
rated contest during the last month.

param include_retired

Include users that have not been online during
the last month.

param contest_id

ID of the contest. It's not the round number.
It can be seen in the contest URL. For example, in the following
,
the 566 is the contest ID.

return

A list of users.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.user.User.user_rating
documentation:
Retrieves the rating changes of a certain user over time.

```
param handle

User's codeforces handle.

return

A list of the user's rating changes.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.user.User.user_status
documentation:
Retrieves the submissions of a specific user.

```
param handle

User's codeforces handle.

param _from

1-based index of the first submission of the list that is
going to be retrieved.

param count

Number of submissions to return.

return

A list of the user's submissions.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.contest.Contest.contest_hacks
documentation:
Fetches hacks created during a contest.

Full information about hacks is available only after some time after
the contest ends. During the contest, a user can only view his own
hacks.

```
param contest_id

ID of the contest. It's not the round number.
It can be seen in the contest URL. For example, in the following
,
the 566 is the contest ID.

param as_manager

If set to true, the response will contain
information available to contest managers. Otherwise, the response
will contain only the information available to the participants.
You must be a contest manager to use it.

return

A list of hacks made during the requested contest.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.contest.Contest.contest_list
documentation:
Retrieves the list of upcoming/finished contests.

```
param gym

If true, then gym contests are returned.
Otherwise, regular contests are returned.

return

A list containing upcoming/finished contests.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.contest.Contest.contest_rating_changes
documentation:
Retrieves rating changes during a specific contest.

```
param contest_id

ID of the contest. It's not the round number.
It can be seen in the contest URL. For example, in the following
,
the 566 is the contest ID.

return

A list of rating changes that happened in the requested
contest.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.contest.Contest.contest_standings
documentation:
Retrieves standings of a specific contest.

This can return the standings of specific users using the `handles`
parameter.

```
param contest_id

ID of the contest. It's not the round number.
It can be seen in the contest URL. For example, in the following
,
the 566 is the contest ID.

param as_manager

If set to true, the response will contain
information available to contest managers. Otherwise, the response
will contain only the information available to the participants.
You must be a contest manager to use it.

param _from

1-based index of the first standings row that is going
to be fetched. (Note: If you request specific users using ,
then this will indicate from where it should start searching)

param count

Number of rows to return.

param handles

List of handles. The API will not return information
for more than 10000 handles.

param room

If specified, then only participants from this room will
be shown in the result.

param show_unofficial

If true, then all participants are shown.
Otherwise, only official contestants are shown.

param participant_types

List of allowed participant types. Only
participants with the specified types will be returned.

return

A list of standings of the requested contest.
```

methods.name: cforces.methods.contest.Contest.contest_status
documentation:
Retrieves submissions for a specific contest.

It can also fetch submissions of a specific user in the contest if the
parameter `handle` is used.

```
param contest_id

ID of the contest. It's not the round number.
It can be seen in the contest URL. For example, in the following
,
the 566 is the contest ID.

param as_manager

If set to true, the response will contain
information available to contest managers. Otherwise, the response
will contain only the information available to the participants.
You must be a contest manager to use it.

param handle

If specified, returns the submissions in this contest of
the user with this handle.

param _from

1-based index of the first standings row that is going
to be retrieved.

param count

Number of rows to return.

return

A list of submission made during the requested contest.
```
